title: State-space tyre-state expert with filtered cliff probability
summary: A 2026 F1 tyre-degradation paper shows state-space models with pit-stop resets outperform AR-style baselines and yield interpretable latent pace/degradation states.
rationale: A 2026 F1 tyre-degradation paper shows state-space models with pit-stop resets outperform AR-style baselines and yield interpretable latent pace/degradation states. That fits this task better than more hand-set tyre-life thresholds because pit decisions are triggered by hidden degradation phase changes, not just observed TyreLife.
implementation_hint: Build a lightweight per-(Year,Race,Driver,Stint) online filter using only past laps to estimate latent tyre pace, degradation slope, and probability of entering a late-stint 'cliff' regime; feed those filtered states and one-step-ahead residuals into the current tabular model. Validate with race-held-out folds so the filter never sees future laps from the same stint.
expected_effect: Should reduce variance from noisy LapTime_Delta/Cumulative_Degradation and add signal exactly near ambiguous pit-window laps.
risk: Extra complexity may add little if current features already proxy latent state well; must enforce strict causal filtering to avoid leakage.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
